clusters.title: Musk says he'll appeal dismissal of lawsuit against OpenAI
clusters.neutral_headline: Musk loses OpenAI court battle after jury finds he waited too long to sue
bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, BBC World, France24 and others

items.title: Musk says he'll appeal dismissal of lawsuit against OpenAI
items.standfirst: A California jury on Monday unanimously dismissed Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman. CBS News senior business and technology correspondent Jo Ling Kent reports.
items.content: A California jury on Monday unanimously dismissed Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman. CBS News senior business and technology correspondent Jo Ling Kent reports.

items.title: Musk loses OpenAI court battle after jury finds he waited too long to sue
items.standfirst: Jurors spent weeks hearing about Musk's claim that Altman had "stolen a charity."
items.content: Jurors spent weeks hearing about Musk's claim that Altman had "stolen a charity."

items.title: Jury unanimously dismisses Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI
items.standfirst: California jury dismissed all charges, finding that Musk missed the three-year statute of limitations to file suit.
items.content: California jury dismissed all charges, finding that Musk missed the three-year statute of limitations to file suit.

items.title: Elon Musk loses lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman
items.standfirst: Tech billionaire Elon Musk has lost his $160 billion dollar lawsuit against OpenAI and its chief Sam Altman in a federal court in California. After 11 days of testimonies and arguments, a 9-member jury deliberated for less than two hours to reach a unanimous verdict, saying Musk filed the case too late, beyond the statute of limitations. Also on the show, research finds US drivers have spent over $40 billion in additional fuel costs since the start of the Iran war.
items.content: Tech billionaire Elon Musk has lost his $160 billion dollar lawsuit against OpenAI and its chief Sam Altman in a federal court in California. After 11 days of testimonies and arguments, a 9-member jury deliberated for less than two hours to reach a unanimous verdict, saying Musk filed the case too late, beyond the statute of limitations. Also on the show, research finds US drivers have spent over $40 billion in additional fuel costs since the start of the Iran war.

items.title: Elon Musk just lost another lawsuit. Will he keep fighting?
items.standfirst: Musk's loss against OpenAI is the latest in a string of courtroom defeats.
items.content: Musk's loss against OpenAI is the latest in a string of courtroom defeats.

items.title: Federal court rejects Elon Musk's claims against OpenAI, saying he filed his lawsuit too late
items.standfirst: A federal jury ruled Monday that Elon Musk waited too long to sue OpenAI and its co-founders, delivering a decisive victory to the ChatGPT startup and ending one of Silicon Valley's most closely watched courtroom battles. The swift decision caps a three-week trial that saw a parade of tech titans take the stand, with Musk arguing that OpenAI's pivot to a profit-driven business betrayed its original nonprofit mandate. FRANCE 24's Wassim Cornet reports from Los Angeles.
items.content: A federal jury ruled Monday that Elon Musk waited too long to sue OpenAI and its co-founders, delivering a decisive victory to the ChatGPT startup and ending one of Silicon Valley's most closely watched courtroom battles. The swift decision caps a three-week trial that saw a parade of tech titans take the stand, with Musk arguing that OpenAI's pivot to a profit-driven business betrayed its original nonprofit mandate. FRANCE 24's Wassim Cornet reports from Los Angeles.

items.title: Musk loses landmark lawsuit against OpenAI after jury finds he filed his claim too late
items.standfirst: The world's richest man Elon Musk lost his blockbuster lawsuit against artificial intelligence giant OpenAI on Monday, with a federal jury finding that the tycoon had waited too long to bring his case forward. The trial saw some of the most powerful figures in Silicon Valley go head-to-head with their competing ambitions for the rapidly changing technology.
items.content: The world's richest man Elon Musk lost his blockbuster lawsuit against artificial intelligence giant OpenAI on Monday, with a federal jury finding that the tycoon had waited too long to bring his case forward. The trial saw some of the most powerful figures in Silicon Valley go head-to-head with their competing ambitions for the rapidly changing technology.

items.title: Jury Says Elon Musk Sued Sam Altman Too Late Over OpenAI
items.standfirst: A federal jury rejects Elon Musk's claims about OpenAI under Sam Altman and says he waited too long to sue. Musk took Altman to trial saying he went against OpenAI's mission by turning it into a for-profit business. Matthew Schettenhelm of Bloomberg Intelligence has been following the case and has more. (Source: Bloomberg)
items.content: A federal jury rejects Elon Musk's claims about OpenAI under Sam Altman and says he waited too long to sue. Musk took Altman to trial saying he went against OpenAI's mission by turning it into a for-profit business. Matthew Schettenhelm of Bloomberg Intelligence has been following the case and has more. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Jury dismisses all claims in Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI CEO Sam Altman
items.standfirst: Musk had sought to oust Altman from his leadership position over claims that he and others breached their duty to OpenAI's original nonprofit mission and unjustly enriched themselves.
items.content: Musk had sought to oust Altman from his leadership position over claims that he and others breached their duty to OpenAI's original nonprofit mission and unjustly enriched themselves.(Image credit: Godofredo A. Vásquez)

items.content: Elon Musk has lost his lawsuit against OpenAI and its chief executive Sam Altman.

items.title: Elon Musk Loses Case Against Sam Altman Over OpenAI’s Overhaul
items.standfirst: A jury rejected Elon Musk’s claims that OpenAI under Sam Altman’s leadership betrayed its mission to benefit the public by morphing into a for-profit business, finding that he waited too long to sue the company.
items.content: A jury rejected Elon Musk’s claims that OpenAI under Sam Altman’s leadership betrayed its mission to benefit the public by morphing into a for-profit business, finding that he waited too long to sue the company.

items.title: Elon Musk Loses Landmark Lawsuit Against OpenAI
items.standfirst: The nine-member panel took only two hours to return a verdict in favor of OpenAI on Monday, which the judge quickly adopted as her own final decision.
items.content: The nine-member panel took only two hours to return a verdict in favor of OpenAI on Monday, which the judge quickly adopted as her own final decision.

items.title: Trump drops $10 billion IRS lawsuit in exchange for $1.7 billion fund for allies
items.standfirst: The US Justice Department on Monday announced the creation of a $1.7 billion fund to compensate allies of President Donald Trump who believe they were politically persecuted by the department under his predecessor Joe Biden's term in office. The fund is part of a deal to bring an end to Trump's $10 billion lawsuit against the Internal Revenue Service for the leak of his tax returns during his first stint in the White House.
items.content: The US Justice Department on Monday announced the creation of a $1.7 billion fund to compensate allies of President Donald Trump who believe they were politically persecuted by the department under his predecessor Joe Biden's term in office. The fund is part of a deal to bring an end to Trump's $10 billion lawsuit against the Internal Revenue Service for the leak of his tax returns during his first stint in the White House.

items.title: Judge dismisses Trump's IRS lawsuit, paving the way for a settlement
items.standfirst: The president sued the IRS and the Treasury Department in January, demanding $10 billion over the leak of his tax returns years ago.

clusters.title: US tightens Ebola precautions as outbreak declared international emergency
clusters.neutral_headline: At least 100 dead in Ebola outbreak in DR Congo, official says
bullet_summary: M​edical personnel were rushing on Monday to the frontlines of a fast-spreading Ebola outbreak in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo, with cases also confirmed in Uganda
The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo has grown to nearly 500 suspected cases and 116 deaths, the United Nations said Monday

items.title: US tightens Ebola precautions as outbreak declared international emergency
items.standfirst: The United States said Monday it is bolstering precautions to prevent the spread of Ebola, including screening air travellers from outbreak-hit areas and temporarily suspending visa services, as the World Health Organization declares the outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo an international health emergency.
items.content: The United States said Monday it is bolstering precautions to prevent the spread of Ebola, including screening air travellers from outbreak-hit areas and temporarily suspending visa services, as the World Health Organization declares the outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo an international health emergency.

items.title: DRC Ebola outbreak 'way bigger that what we see now', virologist says
items.standfirst: M​edical personnel were rushing on Monday to the frontlines of a fast-spreading Ebola outbreak in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo, with cases also confirmed in Uganda. FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney speaks with Muhammad Munir, Professor in Virology at Lancaster University. He says that "the scale of the outbreak is way bigger than what we're having now and that is the reason the WHO decided on a public health emergency of international concern".
items.content: M​edical personnel were rushing on Monday to the frontlines of a fast-spreading Ebola outbreak in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo, with cases also confirmed in Uganda. FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney speaks with Muhammad Munir, Professor in Virology at Lancaster University. He says that "the scale of the outbreak is way bigger than what we're having now and that is the reason the WHO decided on a public health emergency of international concern".

items.title: What to know about the Ebola outbreak
items.standfirst: The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo has grown to nearly 500 suspected cases and 116 deaths, the United Nations said Monday.
items.content: The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo has grown to nearly 500 suspected cases and 116 deaths, the United Nations said Monday.

items.title: At least 100 dead in Ebola outbreak in DR Congo, official says
items.standfirst: The World Health Organization (WHO) has declared the outbreak an international emergency.
items.content: The World Health Organization (WHO) has declared the outbreak an international emergency.

items.title: Ebola: DR Congo outbreak a global health emergency, says WHO
items.standfirst: An outbreak of a rare strain of the Ebola virus in DR Congo was declared “a public health emergency of international concern” by the World Health Organization on Sunday. The outbreak in the eastern Ituri province has already caused at least 91 deaths, with around 350 suspected cases.
items.content: An outbreak of a rare strain of the Ebola virus in DR Congo was declared “a public health emergency of international concern” by the World Health Organization on Sunday. The outbreak in the eastern Ituri province has already caused at least 91 deaths, with around 350 suspected cases.

items.title: Sen. Bill Cassidy loses primary. And, WHO declares Ebola outbreak a global emergency
items.standfirst: Sen. Bill Cassidy, who voted to convict Trump, lost the Republican primary in Louisiana. And, the WHO has declared a global health emergency over a new Ebola outbreak.
items.content: Sen. Bill Cassidy, who voted to convict Trump, lost the Republican primary in Louisiana. And, the WHO has declared a global health emergency over a new Ebola outbreak.(Image credit: Tyler Kaufman)

clusters.title: 3 killed in shooting at Islamic Center of San Diego, police say; 2 suspects dead
clusters.neutral_headline: Gunmen kill 3 people at San Diego Islamic centre in suspected hate crime
bullet_summary: Three people were killed in a shooting at the Islamic Center of San Diego, and two suspected shooters were found dead inside a vehicle nearby, police said
Two teenage gunmen opened fire at an Islamic centre in San Diego on Monday, killing three men before taking their own lives nearby, police said
Two suspects, aged 17 and 19, were also dead from apparent self-inflicted gunshot wounds, officials said
The FBI said it was looking for information from the public as it investigated the shooting

items.title: Three killed in attack at San Diego Islamic centre in suspected hate crime
items.standfirst: Two teenage gunmen opened fire at an Islamic centre in San Diego on Monday, killing three men before taking their own lives nearby, police said. Authorities are investigating the attack as a hate crime, with officials saying a mosque security guard likely prevented further casualties.

items.title: American tests positive for Ebola as outbreak spreads in Africa
items.standfirst: An American aid worker exposed to Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo has tested positive and is being transferred to Germany for treatment, according to the CDC. The U.S. says six additional exposed citizens will also be evacuated. NBC News’ Maggie Vespa reports.

clusters.title: French cinema faces reckoning as media mogul Bolloré blacklists stars for daring to challenge him
clusters.neutral_headline: 'A naive view of regulation of politics in media' in France, expert says
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, Guardian Business

items.title: French cinema faces reckoning as media mogul Bolloré blacklists stars for daring to challenge him
items.standfirst: France's film industry is reeling after its biggest production company – Canal+, owned by right-wing billionaire Vincent Bolloré – announced it would no longer work with some 600 professionals who signed a petition opposing Bolloré and the "grip of the far right" on cinema. The fallout is highlighting some uncomfortable truths about the industry's reliance on one company.
items.content: France's film industry is reeling after its biggest production company – Canal+, owned by right-wing billionaire Vincent Bolloré – announced it would no longer work with some 600 professionals who signed a petition opposing Bolloré and the "grip of the far right" on cinema. The fallout is highlighting some uncomfortable truths about the industry's reliance on one company.

items.title: 'A naive view of regulation of politics in media' in France, expert says
items.standfirst: The head of France's biggest film producer, Canal+, said Sunday that the group would no longer work with 600 industry professionals who signed a petition against right-wing billionaire owner Vincent Bolloré. Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Renaud Foucart, Senior Lecturer in Economics at Lancaster University, highlights the risk of media concentration, saying that "France has always perhaps had a naive view of regulation of politics in media".
items.content: The head of France's biggest film producer, Canal+, said Sunday that the group would no longer work with 600 industry professionals who signed a petition against right-wing billionaire owner Vincent Bolloré. Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Renaud Foucart, Senior Lecturer in Economics at Lancaster University, highlights the risk of media concentration, saying that "France has always perhaps had a naive view of regulation of politics in media".

items.title: France’s top film producer says it will blacklist figures who petitioned against rightwing billionaire
items.standfirst: Canal+ head says he will not work with hundreds of actors and directors who signed protest against Vincent Bolloré’s political swayThe head of France’s biggest film producer, Canal+, has said the group will no longer work with hundreds of cinema figures who signed a petition voicing concern over the growing influence of the rightwing billionaire owner Vincent Bolloré.The open letter, published earlier this week to coincide with the opening of the Cannes film festival, was signed by more than 600 figures, including the actor-director Juliette Binoche, the director and photographer Raymond Depardon, the French-Iranian film-maker Sepideh Farsi and the director Arthur Harari, who co-wrote the Oscar-winning Anatomy of a Fall and is premiering his film The Unknown in the main competition in Cannes. Continue reading...
items.content: Canal+ head says he will not work with hundreds of actors and directors who signed protest against Vincent Bolloré’s political swayThe head of France’s biggest film producer, Canal+, has said the group will no longer work with hundreds of cinema figures who signed a petition voicing concern over the growing influence of the rightwing billionaire owner Vincent Bolloré.The open letter, published earlier this week to coincide with the opening of the Cannes film festival, was signed by more than 600 figures, including the actor-director Juliette Binoche, the director and photographer Raymond Depardon, the French-Iranian film-maker Sepideh Farsi and the director Arthur Harari, who co-wrote the Oscar-winning Anatomy of a Fall and is premiering his film The Unknown in the main competition in Cannes. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

Continue reading...

items.content: The cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak has arrived in the Netherlands and entered the Dutch port of Rotterdam for disinfection.

items.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship arrives in Netherlands, ending long and deadly voyage
items.standfirst: The hantavirus-stricken cruise ship arrived Monday in the Netherlands, ending a seven-week, 8,500-mile journey that was hit by up to 11 infections and three deaths from the rare disease
items.content: The hantavirus-stricken cruise ship arrived Monday in the Netherlands, ending a seven-week, 8,500-mile journey that was hit by up to 11 infections and three deaths from the rare disease

items.title: Nine UK citizens exposed to hantavirus returning to Britain
items.standfirst: Nine people connected to the MV Hondius cruise ship, which was hit by a hantavirus outbreak, are set to arrive in the UK.
items.content: Nine people connected to the MV Hondius cruise ship, which was hit by a hantavirus outbreak, are set to arrive in the UK.

clusters.title: Driver of crashed train tested positive for drugs, Thai police say
clusters.neutral_headline: Driver of crashed train tested positive for drugs, Thai police say
bullet_summary: Reported by BBC World, NBC World

items.title: Driver of crashed train tested positive for drugs, Thai police say
items.standfirst: The freight train collided with a bus in Bangkok, killing eight people and injuring dozens of others.
items.content: The freight train collided with a bus in Bangkok, killing eight people and injuring dozens of others.

items.title: Train driver charged after crash in Bangkok kills 8
items.standfirst: Thai police have charged ​a train driver with negligence after a crash in central Bangkok in which a freight train collided with a public bus.
items.content: Thai police have charged ​a train driver with negligence after a crash in central Bangkok in which a freight train collided with a public bus.
